rulings.Case Title: MELISSA BETTENCOURT VS. CHRISTOPHER J. HARNEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Entire Action And For Entry Of Judgment As To Plaintiffs' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 13, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER HARNEY Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Entire Action And For Entry Of Judgment As To Plaintiffs' Complaint is GRANTED as to defendant Harney only. Defendant's Harney's demurrer was sustained with leave to amend and no Amended Complaint was filed by the Plaintiff. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 13, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ARTHUR TRAYNOR DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The parties must meet and confer prior to filing of the demurrer in person or over the phone. The re-notice of the demurrer set for August 26, 2024 is also ordered off calendar as it is set in violation of CRC 3.1320. In the future all parties must comply with the meet and confer requirements per CCP 430.41 and any demurrer must be set for hearing in accordance with CRC 3.1320. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SARAH LEWIS VS. KENNETH RUNYON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 13, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ENA REALES, LUIS MAGALLON DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege extreme and outrageous behavior by the moving defendants. New factual allegations must be in bold font and must be included within the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Demurrer to the cause of action for conspiracy is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege all elements of conspiracy (including formation) within the general allegations and not as a separate cause of action. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Remaining causes of action against the moving defendants are sufficiently stated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE VS. ZACHARY HOWITT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses From Zachary Howitt To Form Interrogs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 13, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF GAETANI REAL ESTATE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses From Zachary Howitt To Form Interrogs is continued to August 20, 2024 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief addressing (1) the service of discovery on "Howitt Brown," not a party to this action. See Bornstein Declaration paragraph 3; and (2) providing legal authority for the assertion that responses to discovery served by mail to a New York address on July 1, 2024 were due on July 11, 2024. See Bornstein Declaration paragraph 4. Supplemental brief/declaration shall not exceed two pages and shall be filed and served no later than August 14, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MVP I, LLC VS. JASON HUNT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 13, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT JASON HUNT MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. "Personal jurisdiction is conferred by service on the tenant of the unlawful detainer summons and complaint." Borsuk v. Appellate Division of Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 607, 612. Additionally, complaint is properly verified. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
